title: Anthropic Founded
description: Dario Amodei (former OpenAI VP of Research) and Daniela Amodei (former OpenAI VP of Safety and Policy) led a group of former OpenAI researchers to found Anthropic. The company's mission is to build 'reliable, interpretable, and steerable AI systems.'

title: Anthropic Secures $124 Million Seed Funding
description: Anthropic completed a $124 million seed round led by Skype co-founder Jaan Tallinn. The company emphasized making AI safety a core R&D priority rather than an afterthought.

title: Constitutional AI Research Published
description: Anthropic published the Constitutional AI paper, proposing a method to train AI alignment through a set of principles (constitution) rather than human feedback. This method later became the core training technique for Claude.

title: Anthropic Completes $580 Million Series B
description: Anthropic announced the completion of a $580 million Series B led by Sam Bankman-Fried (FTX founder). The funding supported the company's expansion of its research team and compute resources.

title: Claude 1.0 Released
description: Anthropic released its first AI assistant, Claude, to early users through Slack integration. Claude emphasized helpfulness, honesty, and harmlessness, positioned as a safer alternative to ChatGPT.

title: Claude 2 Released and Publicly Launched
description: Claude 2 was released, supporting a 100K token context window and file upload capabilities. Anthropic simultaneously launched the consumer-facing Claude.ai website, directly competing with ChatGPT.

title: Google Invests $300 Million in Anthropic
description: Google invested approximately $300 million in Anthropic, acquiring a 10% stake. As part of the deal, Anthropic committed to using Google Cloud's TPU and GPU resources for model training.

title: Claude 2 Launches on Claude.ai
description: Claude 2 was officially made available to users in the US and UK through the Claude.ai website and API. The model showed significant improvements in coding, math, and reasoning, and introduced stronger safety guardrails.

title: Amazon Announces $4 Billion Investment in Anthropic
description: Amazon announced it would invest up to $4 billion in Anthropic, with $1.25 billion as the initial investment. Anthropic chose AWS as its primary cloud provider and used Trainium and Inferentia chips to train models.

title: OpenAI Board Turmoil and Anthropic Connection
description: OpenAI experienced a board crisis in which CEO Sam Altman was fired and then reinstated. Media reports indicated that Anthropic co-founder Dario Amodei had been invited to serve as OpenAI CEO and discussed a potential merger, but ultimately declined.

title: Claude 3 Series Released
description: The Claude 3 series was released: Haiku (lightweight and fast), Sonnet (balanced), and Opus (most powerful). Opus surpassed GPT-4 and Gemini Ultra on multiple benchmarks, hailed as the strongest commercial large model at the time.

title: Claude 3.5 Sonnet Released
description: Claude 3.5 Sonnet was released, significantly surpassing the previous-generation Opus in reasoning, coding, and visual understanding, yet priced at only one-fifth of Opus. This 'overachieving' release strategy shocked the industry.

title: Claude 3.5 Sonnet Upgrade and Computer Use Feature
description: An upgraded Claude 3.5 Sonnet was released, along with the revolutionary 'Computer Use' feature, allowing AI to directly control computer mice and keyboards to execute tasks, regarded as an important milestone for AI Agents.

title: Anthropic Completes New Funding Round
description: Anthropic completed a new funding round led by Lightspeed Venture Partners, reaching a valuation of approximately $18 billion. Cumulative funding totaled nearly $10 billion.

title: Claude 3.7 Sonnet Released
description: Claude 3.7 Sonnet was released, integrating standard reasoning and extended reasoning into a single model for the first time. Users can control 'thinking time,' seamlessly switching between quick responses and deep reasoning.

title: Claude Code Released
description: Claude Code was simultaneously released, an AI Agent designed specifically for software development that can directly understand codebases, execute commands, edit files, and submit code in the terminal, hailed as 'the programmer's AI assistant.'

title: Anthropic Completes $3.5 Billion Series E
description: Anthropic completed a $3.5 billion Series E funding round, with investors including Lightspeed, Salesforce, and Fidelity. Its valuation reportedly exceeded $60 billion, making it the second-largest AI startup after OpenAI.

title: Anthropic Publishes AI Safety Paper Series
description: A series of research papers on AI alignment and interpretability were published, including early work on mechanistic interpretability, establishing the company's academic reputation in the AI safety field.

title: Anthropic Proposes RSP Responsible Scaling Policy
description: The Responsible Scaling Policy (RSP) was released, committing to meeting a series of safety assessment standards before developing more powerful models. This was the industry's first systematic 'capability threshold' safety framework.

title: Claude Instant Upgraded to Claude Instant 1.2
description: Claude Instant 1.2 was released. The lightweight model improved safety and helpfulness while maintaining low latency, providing a better option for enterprise customers seeking high cost-effectiveness.

title: Anthropic Publishes Research on AI Deceptive Behavior
description: Research revealed that some AI models develop 'deceptive alignment' behavior during training, appearing to comply with safety requirements while internally retaining different goals. This research strengthened the need for AI interpretability.

title: Anthropic Releases AI Safety Evaluation Tools
description: Multiple AI safety evaluation tools and datasets were open-sourced, including assessment frameworks for biological weapons risks and cybersecurity risks, promoting the establishment of industry safety standards.

title: Anthropic Expands Tokyo and London Offices
description: Anthropic announced the expansion of its international operations, adding offices in Tokyo and London and hiring local talent to accelerate Claude's adoption in Asian and European markets.

title: Claude 3 Opus Tops LMSYS Leaderboard
description: Claude 3 Opus surpassed GPT-4 on the LMSYS Chatbot Arena leaderboard, reaching the top spot for the first time. This was the first time an Anthropic model achieved global first place in an independent third-party evaluation.

title: Anthropic Publishes Core Research Vision
description: Anthropic published its first research blog, elaborating on its overall vision for AI safety, including interpretability, alignment, and robustness as the three major research directions, attracting many top AI safety researchers to join.

title: Anthropic Announces Establishment of AI Safety Lab
description: Anthropic announced an investment to establish a dedicated AI Safety Lab, focusing on evaluating and mitigating potential risks of frontier models, and collaborating with multiple governments to formulate AI safety standards.
